fail_fast.description: Wrap multi-line shell scripts with `set -e` so any failed command aborts the action. Defaults to True for multi-line `run:` blocks; set to False to opt out (legacy behavior). Single-line commands ignore this field.
fail_fast.title: Fail Fast

ActionWhen.description: Conditional execution for an action or model deployment
ActionWhen.title: ActionWhen

BinaryConfig.description:
Binary/Package deployment configuration for reCamera C++ applications

Supports:
- .deb package installation via opkg
- Model file deployment to /userdata/local/models
- SysVinit init script deployment
- MQTT external access configuration
- Conflicting service management
BinaryConfig.title: BinaryConfig

ConfigFlowField.description: A field to submit in the HA config flow form
ConfigFlowField.title: ConfigFlowField

DeviceDetectionFailure.description: User-facing remediation message when a hardware detection probe fails.
DeviceDetectionFailure.title: DeviceDetectionFailure

DeviceDetectionProbe.description:
A single hardware-identity probe injected by a device-class profile.

Probes opt-in: profile authors add them when the wrong hardware would
cause real damage (e.g. Jetson profile's iptables-raw fix would prompt
sudo and bounce Docker on a developer's Mac). When a probe is present,
the deployer runs it before any pre_checks or `actions.before`, and
aborts with `on_failure.message` if the command's exit code is non-zero.

Today only ``type: shell`` is supported — extending to ``http`` /
``file_exists`` etc. is a future patch; keep the schema discriminated
so we can add union variants without breaking existing profiles.
DeviceDetectionProbe.title: DeviceDetectionProbe

DockerRemoteConfig.description: Remote Docker deployment configuration via SSH
DockerRemoteConfig.title: DockerRemoteConfig

FileUploadConfig.description:
File to download locally and upload to device via SFTP.

Use this when the target device may not have internet access.
The provisioning station downloads the file first, then uploads via SSH.
FileUploadConfig.title: FileUploadConfig

FirmwareVariant.description:
A firmware override variant.

Used in two places, with identical semantics:

* ``FirmwareConfig.variants`` — keyed by chip (e.g. ``esp32s3``,
  ``esp32c6``); the esp32 deployer swaps in the matching variant once the
  chip is detected.
* ``FirmwareConfig.language_variants`` — keyed by App-UI language code
  (e.g. ``zh`` / ``en``); the esp32 deployer swaps in the matching variant
  based on the language passed through from the App, *after* the chip
  variant so language overrides win on shared fields.

All fields are optional and additive: a variant overrides only the fields
it sets. ``source`` lets a variant point at a different base firmware file
entirely (generic — not tied to any one solution).
FirmwareVariant.title: FirmwareVariant

RobotInspectConfig.description:
Configuration for the ``robot_inspect`` verify step.

Platform side is intentionally stateless — no Robot instances, no
sessions, no long-lived connections. The platform only:

1. Polls the user-supplied ``endpoint`` (typically the solution's main
   container exposing ``GET /observation``) at ``poll_hz``.
2. Renders the resulting JSON observation against the schema fetched
   from ``schema_endpoint`` (typically ``GET /observation/schema``).
3. Falls back to ``fallback_message`` when the endpoint is unreachable.

Both URLs may contain ``{{var}}`` template placeholders (e.g.
``{{deploy.host}}`` / ``{{deploy.observation_port}}``); resolution
happens in the frontend before the URL is handed to the backend
reverse proxy.
RobotInspectConfig.title: RobotInspectConfig

RobotInspectField.description:
Optional per-field rendering hint for the robot_inspect verify panel.

The schema fetched at runtime from the device's
``GET /observation/schema`` endpoint is the source of truth; this model
only provides solution-author overrides (display label, value range,
etc.) for fields the platform cannot infer from the live schema alone.
RobotInspectField.title: RobotInspectField

WebDashboardConfig.description:
Configuration for web_dashboard verification type.

Verifies a deployment succeeded by pointing the user to a web URL
they can open (Grafana, Frigate, Node-RED Dashboard, etc.). The
dashboard itself IS the verification — if the page loads with data,
the deployment worked.

Frontend behavior:
    - Renders the URL as visible, copyable text (so the user can paste
      it elsewhere or recover if the auto-open fails)
    - Auto-opens via `openExternal()` in the user's default system
      browser (Tauri shell.open in desktop app, window.open in dev)
    - Never embedded in an iframe or in-app webview — heavy dashboards
      like Grafana work better in a real browser with cookies/devtools

Supports `{{var}}` templating against preceding step's user_inputs
(e.g. `http://{{host}}:3000`).
WebDashboardConfig.title: WebDashboardConfig
